clusters.title: Sri Lanka’s missing Balance Sheet

versions.title: Sri Lanka’s missing Balance Sheet
versions.deck: Sri Lanka’s crisis is often described in familiar terms: excessive borrowing, weak fiscal discipline and unsustainable debt. This diagnosis is not wrong. But it is incomplete. At its core, the crisis reflects a deeper issue in how sovereign financial positions are assessed and managed. Fiscal analysis remains overwhelmingly focused on flows – deficits, debt servicing and financing needs – while the stock of assets underpinning the public sector

clusters.title: Energy supplies are crashing: What must Sri Lanka do?

versions.title: Energy supplies are crashing: What must Sri Lanka do?
versions.deck: According to Kpler, a global business intelligence company, seaborne crude and condensate exports were down 6.9 million bpd in March compared to the previous month, and are down another 1.8 million bpd through 20 April. This leads to the conclusion that loadings are likely to be down 8.8 million bpd from prewar levels. versions.title: Choosing disaster over prevention and mitigation: Wilful blindness or short-sighted idiocy?
versions.deck: Sri Lanka has been a serial victim of a brutal cocktail of natural and man-made macro disasters in recent times. These twin forces have not only claimed thousands of lives but have systematically crippled the country’s economic foundation, exposing a dangerous void in proactive national planning. versions.title: Underdog Asanga wins NOC SL Presidency beating hyped-up Jaswar, Shirantha
versions.deck: Multiple sports personality Asanga Seneviratne on Saturday clinched the Presidency of the National Olympic Committee of Sri Lanka (NOC SL), beating the much hyped-up Shirantha Peiris and Jaswar Umar. versions.title: Govt. flags negligence and vulnerability in Treasury payments after $ 2.5 m theft
versions.deck: Industry and Entrepreneurship Development Deputy Minister Chathuranga Abeysinghe has pointed to “negligence and vulnerability in Treasury payments” following the $ 2.5 million cyber breach linked to a Government debt transaction, framing the incident as part of broader systemic weaknesses in public sector processes.

clusters.title: Sri Lanka display dominance over Bangladesh

versions.title: Sri Lanka display dominance over Bangladesh
versions.deck: Sri Lanka Women displayed their dominance over Bangladesh Women in the ODI series when they won their second bilateral series against them 2-1 at Rajshahi. The two countries had met on an earlier occasion in a bilateral series three years ago in Sri Lanka when the home team emerged victorious 1-0.
